Given this environment, better approach: a script file that does the logic. We can create a script file via write_file then execute it. Since we can only use tools, but can we create a script file? Yes. Use write_file to create a script in /tmp maybe. Then run it. Do we need root? Yes.

We create /tmp/openssl_pkg_install.sh with the script. Then run it. That may keep command short.

We'll write the script content:

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

if ! rpm -q --quiet kernel-core; then
    echo "kernel-core missing, skipping"
    exit 0
fi
if grep -q s390x /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease || grep -q s390x /proc/sys/kernel/arch; then
    echo "Unsupported architecture; skipping"
    exit 0
fi

dnf makecache --quiet
if ! dnf list available openssl-pkcs11 > /dev/null; then
    echo "openssl-pkcs11 not available"
    exit 0
fi

if ! rpm -q --quiet openssl-pkcs11; then
    dnf -y install openssl-pkcs11
fi

echo "openssl-pkcs11 installation check complete"

Now to write file, then run it. We'll set mode 755.
